title: Mastering Semantic HTML and Modern CSS
summary: Solidify your foundation in semantic HTML5 and modern CSS to build accessible, responsive, and maintainable web interfaces.
personalizationNote: Focus on semantic structure and CSS best practices since you have core web development experience but your HTML/CSS skills need sharpening.
videoStyle: Clean code demos with example websites
learningObjectives: Identify semantic HTML elements and their use cases
Implement responsive layouts with Flexbox and Grid
Apply best practices for scalable CSS architecture
Enhance accessibility via HTML attributes and ARIA roles
knowledgeChecks: Which semantic HTML tag best represents the main content area?
Name two CSS layout methods for responsive design.
What is an ARIA role used for?
narrationScript:
Scene 1: Why Semantic HTML Matters
Semantic HTML5 elements like <header>, <main>, and <footer> give meaning to your content, boosting SEO and accessibility without extra effort.

Scene 2: Modern CSS Layouts: Flexbox & Grid
Use Flexbox to align items in a row or column, and Grid to create complex layouts. Combining them lets you craft dynamic interfaces that work on any screen size.

Scene 3: Scalable and Maintainable CSS Practices
Adopt CSS custom properties for consistent theming and break styles into reusable modules. These habits prevent messy, hard-to-fix style bugs.

Scene 4: Enhancing Accessibility with ARIA & HTML Attributes
Use ARIA roles to define regions for screen readers, and ensure keyboard users can navigate your interface smoothly — simple steps with big impact.

slides.title: Why Semantic HTML Matters
slides.speakerNotes: Let's start by understanding why semantic HTML isn't just a formality. Using the right tags improves SEO, makes your apps accessible, and helps future you maintain code faster.
slides.narration: Semantic HTML5 elements like <header>, <main>, and <footer> give meaning to your content, boosting SEO and accessibility without extra effort.
slides.visualCue: Code snippet highlighting semantic tags with overlay text

slides.title: Modern CSS Layouts: Flexbox & Grid
slides.speakerNotes: CSS has evolved with powerful tools. Flexbox and Grid let you build layouts that adapt effortlessly across devices — a must-have skill for frontend engineers.
slides.narration: Use Flexbox to align items in a row or column, and Grid to create complex layouts. Combining them lets you craft dynamic interfaces that work on any screen size.
slides.visualCue: Animated layout switching between flexbox and grid examples

slides.title: Scalable and Maintainable CSS Practices
bullets: Use CSS variables and custom properties
slides.speakerNotes: Writing CSS that's clean and maintainable lets your team move fast without running into style conflicts. We'll look at variables and modularity techniques.
slides.narration: Adopt CSS custom properties for consistent theming and break styles into reusable modules. These habits prevent messy, hard-to-fix style bugs.

slides.title: Enhancing Accessibility with ARIA & HTML Attributes
slides.speakerNotes: Accessibility ensures everyone can use your apps. Adding ARIA roles and proper attributes makes your UI inclusive and compliant.
slides.narration: Use ARIA roles to define regions for screen readers, and ensure keyboard users can navigate your interface smoothly — simple steps with big impact.
slides.visualCue: Screen reader highlighting ARIA roles with keyboard navigation cues
